title: Project Manager, PMP
description:
TECHEAD is celebrating over thirty-five years of incredible heritage, talent, and accomplishments! To learn more about TECHEAD, visit us at TECHEAD.com or on Glassdoor.




IT Project Manager, PMP | 22048

Contract to Hire

Chesapeake, VA | 100% On-site




**No C2C Candidates**




Are you a seasoned Project Manager with 5 years of experience passionate about leading dynamic teams and delivering exceptional results? We're seeking a highly motivated and skilled individual to join our team and drive high-visibility projects that significantly impact our organization and members.

Reporting to the Director of Project Management, you'll be instrumental in leading, planning, organizing, and motivating Agile or Hybrid project teams. You'll leverage your expertise in project management methodologies to ensure successful outcomes, align with stakeholder needs, and uphold our commitment to quality.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment and can expertly manage several concurrent, complex projects across various business functions. You'll ensure our members receive unwavering, exceptional service that reflects our values and dedication.

What You'll Do:

 * Lead and manage large-scale, enterprise-level projects from initiation to completion.
 * Drive Agile and Hybrid project teams by skillfully planning, managing, and executing project objectives, with a strong emphasis on Agile principles and frameworks like Scrum.
 * Develop and maintain comprehensive project plans, product backlogs, and schedules using Agile tools and techniques, ensuring adaptability to evolving requirements.
 * Ensure timely delivery of project milestones, utilizing Agile ceremonies and metrics to track progress and make necessary adjustments.
 * Apply advanced project management methodologies and reporting technologies to provide clear visibility into project status and performance.
 * Foster team growth by assisting in project team development, holding members accountable, and mentoring them to embrace an empirical approach to complex work.
 * Optimize project capacity by strategically leveraging organizational resources.
 * Proactively identify and resolve conflicts, roadblocks, impediments, and contention within project teams, cultivating a collaborative and supportive environment.
 * Mitigate project risks to successfully achieve project objectives.
 * Ensure effective and timely communication with project teams and stakeholders regarding status, milestones, successes, and risks, utilizing Agile communication channels.
 * Review and approve project testing strategies, approaches, and plans, integrating Agile testing principles and automation.
 * Manage the enterprise change control process.
 * Represent our organization at community events, conferences, and team outings as a proud Chartway representative.
 * Stay ahead of the curve by keeping up-to-date with industry trends, emerging technologies, and best practices to drive innovation.

This role requires all team members to work Eastern Standard Time (EST) hours to ensure seamless alignment with team operations and stakeholders.

What You'll Bring:

Knowledge, Skills, & Abilities:

 * Strong leadership skills with a proven ability to inspire and empower project teams to deliver high-quality results.
 * Exceptional communication skills (verbal and written) that can engage and motivate Agile or Hybrid teams and stakeholders.
 * Superior organizational skills and meticulous attention to detail, capable of managing multiple projects concurrently in a Hybrid environment.
 * In-depth understanding of Project Management principles and best practices.
 * Proficiency in Agile methodologies and frameworks, including Scrum and Kanban.
 * Adept at leading and facilitating Agile ceremonies such as Sprint Planning, Daily Stand-Up, Sprint Review, and Sprint Retrospectives.
 * Strategic thinker who can draw logical conclusions and exercise decisive judgment.
 * Quick and accurate learner with a proactive and independent work ethic.
 * Excellent analytical, personal, and business acumen.
 * High level of interpersonal skills, emotional intelligence, and leadership presence.
 * Innate desire to mentor, coach, collaborate, and work effectively with teams.
 * Ability to manage up and professionally hold senior-level team members accountable.
 * Proficiency in Microsoft tools, including Microsoft Office 365.
 * Experience with Agile Project Management tools such as Jira.
 * Flexibility and willingness to accommodate project implementation needs, including after-hours, evenings, weekends, and holidays, as required.
 * Ability and willingness to travel as necessary.
 * Strong ability to influence without authority.




Education, Experience, & Certification:

 * Education: Bachelor’s degree required or equivalent experience.
 * Experience: 5 to 7 years managing large-scale Financial projects and Agile projects.
 * Certification: Project Management Professional (PMP) preferred; Certified ScrumMaster (CSM) or equivalent preferred.

title: Project Manager
description:
The Lane Construction Corporation is one of America’s leading construction companies, specializing in large, complex civil infrastructure. For nearly 130 years, it has contributed to the development of the country’s vast network of roads, highways, and bridges, including the Interstate Highway System. Lane also specializes in tunnels, metro and railway systems, as well as airports and water and wastewater treatment plants.






Responsibilities



 * Influences the financial performance of a project by monitoring scheduling, forecasting, and cost control efforts.
 * Identifies and mitigates risks to minimize project delays and cost overruns.
 * Conducts regular site visits to monitor progress, quality, and safety compliance.
 * Collaborates with engineers, and other stakeholders to resolve design issues and address any changes.
 * Closely monitors communicates risks, status etc. in project review meetings.
 * Drives the pursuit of claims & change orders, tracks and reports on progress.
 * Oversees procurement or Buy-out process to ensure it adheres to set budget.
 * Ensures the development & use of a CPM schedule throughout the life of the project.
 * Directs project contract management to review, address and resolve contractual issues & claims.

title: Project Manager
companyName: Vortex Companies - Trenchless Infrastructure Rehabilitation Solutions
description:
Want to be part of one of the country’s fastest growing infrastructure businesses? The Vortex Companies, LLC delivers cutting edge trenchless technologies and turnkey services to make the world’s water cleaner and we’re growing every day! We think BIG about solving the world’s toughest challenges in failing water, sewer, and industrial infrastructure.

At Vortex, you will be surrounded by a culture that puts your safety first in all we do. If you like a fast-paced environment, a team structure that provides support, training, and development, then apply today!

We’re currently seeking an experienced Project Manager to oversee all aspects of construction, industrial, and municipal rehabilitation projects, with a strong focus on field operations, crew management, scheduling, and project financials.

Position Summary

The Project Manager is responsible for overseeing field execution and managing project timelines, budgets, and resource planning. This role requires the ability to coordinate daily operations, track multiple active and upcoming projects, and serve as a key liaison between management and field teams. The ideal candidate is highly organized, safety-focused, and experienced in managing construction projects from start to finish.

Duties & Responsibilities


 * Serve as the primary point of contact between management, operations, and field crews
 * Plan and coordinate all elements of construction project execution
 * Develop and maintain project schedules, ensuring alignment with contractor timelines
 * Oversee cost tracking, revenue forecasting, and overall project financial reporting
 * Manage labor planning, resource forecasting, and equipment/material procurement
 * Attend construction planning meetings, project site walks, and client discussions
 * Assist with quality control and uphold safety protocols across all job sites
 * Support field operations by providing direction and addressing on-site needs
 * Ensure timely project completion within budget and QA/QC standards
 * Review and approve daily reports, time logs, and all vendor/customer invoices
 * Identify opportunities for operational improvement and efficiency
 * Other duties as assigned
   
   

Required Skills & Abilities


 * Strong leadership and communication skills
 * Detail-oriented with excellent organization and time management
 * Proficient in Microsoft Office with advanced Excel capabilities
 * Proven ability to manage field teams and support project execution
 * Highly safety-conscious with a proactive, solutions-oriented mindset
 * Strong understanding of project cost control, scheduling, and reporting
   
   

Education & Experience


 * Minimum 3–5 years of construction project management experience
 * Experience in underground infrastructure, trenchless rehabilitation, or municipal/industrial projects is a plus
   
   

Position Type/Expected Hours of Work: This is a full-time, exempt position. Days and hours of work are typically Monday through Friday, but some flexibility is required as job duties demand.

Benefits - we offer a competitive total compensation package including health, vision, and dental insurance, 401K retirement plan, income protection insurance, off the job accident insurance, PTO and more!

title: Project Manager
description:
Project Manager — Enterprise IT & Business Transformation

Contract Duration: 6 Month Contract-to-Hire

Location: Virginia Beach, VA

Work Arrangement: Hybrid (onsite Tuesday/Wed/Thur)

About Us

Endurance IT Services provides Managed IT Services, Cybersecurity Solutions, IT Consulting, and IT Staffing for Direct‑Hire, Contract‑to‑Hire, and Contract placements. We partner with organizations across the region to deliver highly skilled technical talent aligned with business needs and long‑term growth.

Overview

We are seeking an experienced Project Manager to lead high‑visibility, enterprise‑level initiatives that enhance technology capabilities, improve cross‑functional workflows, and support strategic business outcomes.

This role requires a highly motivated leader skilled in Agile, Hybrid, and traditional project management methodologies who can guide diverse teams, partner with stakeholders, and ensure delivery of meaningful business value. The ideal candidate excels in balancing structure with adaptability, translating business needs into actionable project plans, and fostering an environment of continuous improvement and accountability.

The Project Manager will oversee multiple concurrent projects that may span various business functions, ensuring alignment with organizational goals, stakeholder expectations, and quality standards—while reflecting our unwavering commitment to exceptional member service.

Responsibilities


 * Lead and manage large, complex enterprise‑level projects using Agile or Hybrid delivery models
 * Guide Agile or Hybrid project teams by planning, organizing, and executing project objectives using Scrum, Kanban, and other applicable frameworks
 * Partner with Product Owners to maximize product value and maintain well‑prioritized backlogs
 * Develop and manage detailed project plans, schedules, and Agile artifacts to support transparency and adaptability
 * Ensure on‑time delivery of project milestones through effective use of Agile ceremonies, facilitation techniques, and performance metrics
 * Provide clear visibility into project status, risks, and performance through advanced reporting practices
 * Support and coach project teams by fostering accountability, collaboration, and an empirical approach to complex work
 * Proactively identify, manage, and resolve impediments, risks, conflicts, and dependencies
 * Maintain continuous, effective communication with stakeholders regarding progress, milestones, risks, and decision points
 * Review and approve testing strategies, plans, and outcomes, incorporating Agile testing principles and automation where possible
 * Manage and follow enterprise change control procedures
 * Collaborate with leadership and cross‑functional teams to improve organizational capacity for project work
 * Represent the organization at community events, conferences, and team‑building engagements
 * Stay informed on emerging technologies, industry trends, and best practices to support innovation and competitive advantage
 * Perform additional project‑related duties as assigned to support evolving business needs
   
   

Preferred Qualifications


 * PMP certification and/or experience managing enterprise‑level IT or business transformation projects
 * Certified ScrumMaster (CSM) or equivalent Agile certification
 * Experience working within Agile, Hybrid, and Waterfall environments
 * Strong communication, leadership, and stakeholder‑management abilities
 * Ability to influence without authority and manage up to senior leaders
 * Experience using project management and Agile tools such as Jira and Microsoft Office 365
 * Strong problem‑solving skills and advanced organizational capabilities
 * Demonstrated commitment to exceptional service delivery and team collaboration
 * Flexibility to support project implementations, including after‑hours, weekends, and holidays as needed
 * Ability and willingness to travel as necessary
   
   

Minimum Requirements


 * Bachelor’s degree or equivalent professional experience
 * 5–7 years of experience managing large‑scale, mission‑critical, or Agile projects
 * Equivalent combinations of education and experience will be considered

title: Building Envelope Project Manager
description:
We Make Everything Around Us Better

At PRIME AE, that’s not a slogan, it’s how we work.

Every bridge, building, campus, park, and roadway we design exists to improve how people live, move, learn, and connect. Through architecture, engineering, planning, and consulting, we partner with clients to create thoughtful, practical solutions that leave communities stronger than we found them.

If you want your work to matter, and to be surrounded by smart, collaborative people who care about doing things the right way, you’ll feel at home here.

Who We Are

PRIME AE Group, Inc. is a nationally ranked, full-service architecture and engineering firm with 24 offices across 10 states and a team of 500+ professionals. For nearly two decades, we’ve partnered with public and private sector clients across transportation, education, healthcare, municipal, federal, commercial, and recreational markets.

We’re growing, strategically and intentionally. That means expanding our capabilities, investing in our people, and building teams that are positioned for long-term success.

The Opportunity

We are currently seeking an experienced Building Envelope Project Manager to join our growing team. This role offers the opportunity to lead impactful projects, mentor staff, and serve as a trusted advisor to clients across a diverse portfolio of building types. Based in either our Columbus, Jacksonville, or Baltimore offices.

What You’ll Do:

As a Building Envelope Project Manager, you will lead both routine and complex projects from initial assessment through design and construction administration. You’ll balance technical excellence with project management, client communication, and team leadership. This role is ideal for a technically strong professional who enjoys collaboration, problem-solving, and delivering high-quality work.

Key Responsibilities

Project Leadership & Client Engagement


 * Serve as the primary point of contact for clients, leading project communications and coordination.
 * Manage project scope, schedules, budgets, and staffing to ensure successful delivery.
 * Mentor and guide junior staff, supporting professional development and technical growth.
 * Coordinate and oversee subconsultants to ensure alignment with project objectives and deliverables.
   
   

Project Execution & Technical Delivery


 * Lead and actively participate in the preparation of detailed construction drawings and technical specifications related to building envelope systems.
 * Conduct design meetings, material evaluations, and code reviews to ensure technical accuracy and compliance.
 * Perform on-site field assessments and, as required, forensic investigations to evaluate existing building envelope conditions using appropriate testing and diagnostic methods.
 * Prepare clear, well-organized assessment and investigation reports documenting findings, identifying probable root causes, and recommending remedial strategies.
 * Develop Rough Order of Magnitude (ROM) cost estimates to support client decision-making and capital planning.
 * Manage construction administration activities, including RFIs, submittals, change orders, site visits, and project meetings.
 * Maintain thorough documentation of project decisions and communications.
   
   

Collaboration & Business Development


 * Partner with leadership to identify risks, opportunities, and strategies that support project success and profitability.
 * Support proposal development, scope definition, and fee estimates in collaboration with business development and marketing teams.
 * Assist with invoicing and project financial tracking.
 * Represent PRIME AE at client meetings, industry events, and conferences.
   
   

Professional Growth & Thought Leadership


 * Pursue or maintain professional certifications such as RRC, REWC, or RWC.
 * Contribute to technical presentations, articles, or internal knowledge sharing.
 * Participate in professional organizations and support the continued growth of the Building Envelope practice.
   
   

What You’ll Bring


 * Licensed Architect or Professional Engineer (PE) strongly preferred.
 * First professional degree in architecture or engineering, plus:
    * Engineers: 6+ years in architecture/engineering with 2+ years of building envelope consulting, OR equivalent experience.
    * Architects: 8+ years combined academic and professional experience in building envelope practice.

 * Commitment to earning or holding RRC, REWC, or RWC certifications.
 * Proficiency in Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint, Project; AutoCAD/Revit strongly preferred.
 * Excellent communication, writing, and organizational skills.
 * Ability to manage multiple projects independently while meeting strict deadlines.
 * Willingness to conduct roof and façade work using scaffolding, staging, and aerial lifts.
   
   

Experience That Adds Value


 * Exposure to forensic evaluation of moisture intrusion, air leakage, and thermal performance issues.
 * Advanced technical writing skills, with the ability to translate complex findings into clear, client-facing reports.
 * Experience supporting ROM cost estimates or capital planning for building envelope repairs or restoration.
 * Strong attention to detail when documenting field conditions through photographs, field notes, and sketches.
   
   

Why PRIME AE

We don’t just fill positions, we invest in people.

At PRIME AE, you’ll find a supportive, flexible environment where careers are built intentionally, not accidentally. You’ll work on projects that improve everyday life, alongside colleagues who challenge and respect one another.

What sets us apart:


 * Career Growth: Flexible career paths across disciplines, from technical expertise to project and people leadership
 * People-First Culture: We value trust, integrity, and collaboration, and we mean it
 * Meaningful Work: Projects that strengthen communities, infrastructure, and public spaces
 * Growth with Purpose: Strategic expansion backed by strong leadership and investment
 * National Reach, Local Feel: Big-firm resources with the agility and relationships of local offices
   
   

Our Commitment

PRIME AE Group is committed to creating an inclusive environment where differences are respected, voices are heard, and everyone has the opportunity to succeed.

title: Project Manager
description:
Are you ready to take the lead on technically complex projects that build stronger, more resilient communities ? As a Project Manager, you will be at the forefront of driving success, managing a single project contract to ensure seamless execution from start to finish. With your expertise, you will oversee the budget, coordinate project personnel, and maintain a production schedule that guarantees timely delivery and exceptional quality. Collaborate with industry experts and committed teams to align project goals with o wner expectations, adhering to contract specifications and upholding the highest standards.

The City of Virginia Beach picked Flatiron for phase one of a resiliency project that aims to improve stormwater management, protect the fresh water supply and relieve widespread flooding in the neighborhoods of Windsor Woods, Princess Anne Plaza and The Lakes.

Apply now and transform your career with us.

What You Will Be Doing


 * Manages all aspects of the project, including construction, budget, and cash management for a single assigned project to ensure compliance with the project contract and owner needs.
 * Develops and manages the project schedule, pay estimates, project procedures, staffing requirements, and equipment resources needed to complete accurate and timely project production.
 * Assesses initial project contract and supporting documents for completeness. Works with the owner to resolve specification, contract conflict, and scheduling issues.
 * Oversees engineering and scheduling teams to identify and negotiate any proposed change orders, engaging the Area Manager for support as needed.
 * Creates and presents monthly cost and revenue forecasts and reports to Flatiron leadership.
 * Maintains working relationships with Flatiron supporting departments and the project owner to support the project work plan and payment schedule.
 * Operates as the main point of contact for all third-party stakeholders, including landowners, local business owners, and government agencies that need to be engaged during the project.
 * Reviews and approves all internal and external media news releases regarding the project.
 * Ensures project safety reviews and procedures are followed by conducting weekly safety checks and engaging in safety meetings and monthly review meetings. Supports the safety programs of the project and owner safety programs.
 * Reviews and approves staffing and equipment needs for efficient and effective project production.
 * Participates in the dispute resolution process with support from internal legal counsel.
 * Provides input on equipment procurement methods and contracts to ensure efficient and effective project production.
 * Leads project closeout procedures, finalizing contract paperwork, obtaining retainages, and working with management to resolve any project claims. Leads the project debrief cycle to review all aspects of project work and completion.
   
   

What We Are Looking For


 * 10+ years’ experience in construction project management and 5+ years’ experience in managing personnel required.
 * Bachelor’s Degree in Engineering or related field highly regarded.
 * Must hold a valid Driver’s License
 * Strong interpersonal skills developed to engage and retain positive working relationships while effectively delegate task driven responsibilities to project personnel.
 * Strong developed skill to identify, track and manage project risk.
 * Developing ability to efficiently and effectively manage multiple departments and direct reports to retain focus on one project completion goal and timeline.
 * Identifies growth opportunities for project personnel and developed to coach and mentor project personnel.
 * Advanced knowledge of MS Office, including advanced skill working in Excel.
 * Knowledge of Primavera P6 or other scheduling software.
 * Expertise to read, analyze, and interpret common scientific and technical journals, financial reports, and legal documents. Respond to common inquiries or request for changes/claims from subcontractors, management, co-workers, regulatory agencies, or owners. Writes speeches and articles for publication that conform to prescribed style and format. Proven ability to effectively present information to executive management and/or public groups.
 * Comfortable working with mathematical concepts such as probability and statistics, and fundamentals geometry and trigonometry. Applies concepts such as fractions, percentages, ratios, and proportions to practical situations.
 * Identifies problems, collects data, establishes facts, and draws valid conclusions while interpreting an extensive variety of technical instructions furnished in mathematical or diagram form and deal with several abstract and concrete variables .
   
   

Why work for us

Some of the benefits you may be eligible for as an employee are:


 * Comprehensive compensation package and paid time off program
 * Industry leading 401(k)/RRSP
 * Medical/Extended Health Care, Dental, Vision and/or Provincial Medical
 * Wellness benefits & Employee Assistance Program
 * Tuition Reimbursement Program
   
   

We are an EEO/ADA/Veterans employer.

Salary Min

USD $145,000.00/Yr.

Salary Max

USD $160,000.00/Yr.

title: Senior Project Manager
description:
Make your next move an expert one.

At Colliers it’s not our success that sets us apart, it’s how we achieve it. Our people are passionate, take ownership and always do what’s right for our clients, people and communities.

Why Colliers?

Our enterprising environment needs your expertise to facilitate Colliers’ continued growth as an industry leader. Our nimble, decentralized culture can provide you with a wealth of opportunities to learn about our business and quickly gain experience to accelerate your career.

*** Please note this is an onsite position - based in our Norfolk, VA location ***

About You

We’re looking for stellar construction project managers who are super organized, have a keen eye for the details and have managed and assisted in complex construction projects from start to finish – on time and on budget. You’re good at building strong, trusting client relationships and have a knack for bringing the right people together to come up with innovative ideas and solutions.

You love variety in your work and when every day is different from the next. You’re a people person, a relationship builder and see working alongside some of the best Corporate Real Estate brokers on site with their clients as your ideal next role.

In this role, you’ll tackle projects of varying size and complexity both locally and out of market. Are you looking to expand your superior project management skills to make a difference for new clients and businesses? Look no further.

In this role, you will


 * You establish a pathway to project success based upon key performance indexes as defined by the stakeholders.
 * You oversee development of the Project Roadmap, leading the team from the initial project concept through final punch-list and close-out.
 * You create the schedule/budget and “red flag” any possible deviations and resolve any issues as they arise.
 * You develop strong industry relationships and do a great job representing the Colliers project management brand.
 * You will carve out a key role on the ground floor of a developing team and utilize Colliers resources - and brand-new office space - to expand the team’s market and recognition.
   
   

What You Bring


 * Five (5)+ years in Design/Construction/Project Management related field is desirable
 * Extensive knowledge of design process, construction and project management
 * Working knowledge of MS Word, MS Excel, MS Project and industry specific software; i.e. Bluebeam, CAD, or Revit
 * Ability to read and understand architectural plans and technical specifications
 * Ability to lead and direct a variety of vendors, provide critical thinking to problem solving
 * Ability to travel 40% (in state and overnight)
   
   

Bonus Skills And Experience


 * Bachelor's degree or related degree required
 * Related experience in a government environment
 * Experience in directing and maintaining team spirit
 * Familiar with business development strategies and harboring industry relationships
   
   

Make your next move an expert one and join us as we lead the industry into the future.

Applications will be accepted on an ongoing basis.

Direct applicants only please, no agencies.

Colliers respects diversity and is an equal opportunity employer. No employee or applicant for employment will be discriminated against on the basis of any actual or perceived membership in any protected category including race, color, creed, religion, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, sex or gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, related medical conditions and lactation), gender identity or gender expression (including transgender status), sexual orientation, marital status, military service and veteran status, disability, protected medical condition as defined by applicable state or local law, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state, or local laws and ordinances.
